mnemonics: McGraw-Hill Heights: Imagine stacks (LIFO) and queues (FIFO) as different types of hills to climb. McGraw-Hill is known for publishing data structures textbooks, so picture the cover of the book for this module.
C G H Stacks Queues: Core, General, Height (for stacks) & Queues. McGraw-Hill often uses these terms in their data structure chapters.
keyPoints: Stacks are LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) data structures, like a stack of plates.
Queues are FIFO (First-In, First-Out) data structures, like a line of people waiting.
These are fundamental, linear data structures used for managing data in a specific order.
commonMistakes: Confusing LIFO (stack) with FIFO (queue) operation, especially when removing elements.
Forgetting to check for stack underflow (popping an empty stack) or queue underflow (dequeuing an empty queue), leading to errors.
